Keep plateau c7 init at 1.0 since y is min-max normalized to 0..1.

tried_paradigms_summary.idea: Adopt the Oosawa/Knowles closed-form sigmoidal solution structure as the RHS via a logistic equation whose effective rate is an explicit power of m0 and M0, capturing m0-dependent onset analytically

Introduce a Gompertz-type asymmetric saturation by using a logarithmic capacity term instead of linear (plateau - c), giving the long-flat-lag / soft-plateau asymmetry shape loss rewards, while keeping mass-conservation via a positive plateau
Reduce parameter count to a 5-constant Weibull-rate logistic front (better-conditioned least-squares, no redundant gate)
Model the convertible-mass deficit directly with an m0-dependent plateau ceiling AND a sqrt-secondary term (Oosawa square-root autocatalysis) to fix slope profile
Reparameterize the RHS in a dimensionless self-similar form to make constants scale-invariant and robust across proteins (cross-protein eval_only datasets)

active_paradigms.idea: Reparameterize to a dimensionless, scale-invariant Knowles front with FIXED integer kinetic orders (remove the three fragile fitted exponents on m0 and c), keeping the same mass-conserving capacity*growth*gate skeleton

Make the plateau and onset DIMENSIONLESS by normalizing the raw m0/M0 features into bounded saturating ratios (m0/(m0+c)) so one constant set is genuinely transferable across proteins with wildly different concentration units

Replace the additive primary+elongation+secondary sum with a single multiplicative Hill-saturated autocatalytic core that has a built-in non-zero lag-breaking baseline, reducing constant count to improve fit reliability and the slope-profile shape term
